conversation_log.summary: Task is well-structured and thorough, but proposes Docker file locations that contradict the canonical folder structure defined in architecture.md and ADR-004. The architecture specifies: docker-compose.yml at docker/docker-compose.yml (not repo root), Dockerfiles at docker/Dockerfile.frontend and docker/Dockerfile.backend (not apps/*/Dockerfile). ADR-004 Rule #4 states: 'docker/ contains all Docker-related files. No Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml at the repo root.' The task must be updated to place all Docker files in the docker/ directory and adjust volume mount paths, build contexts, and README instructions accordingly. The docker compose up command is run from docker/, not the repo root. All other aspects (service names, networking, env vars, dev-only builds, named volumes) are architecturally sound.

5 acceptance criteria require revision before implementation. (1) AC #1: wrong file path — references repo root instead of docker/docker-compose.yml, contradicting the Architect's required correction. (2) AC #3: wrong working directory — references running from repo root instead of docker/, contradicting the Architect's required correction. (3) AC #6: 'backend reaching the database' is vague and unobservable — no specific verification mechanism (endpoint, log message, exit code) is defined. (4) AC #7: 'frontend can reach backend' is vague and unobservable — no concrete request, URL, or expected response is defined. (5) AC #8: wrong Dockerfile paths — references apps/frontend/Dockerfile and apps/backend/Dockerfile instead of docker/Dockerfile.frontend and docker/Dockerfile.backend, contradicting the Architect's required correction. Additionally, AC #9 and AC #10 are mechanically inaccurate (volume mounts are declared in the compose file, not in Dockerfiles) and should be reworded for test precision, though they are testable against the compose file with interpretation. 8 criteria (AC #2, #4, #5, #11, #12, #13, #14, #15) are testable as written pending the path corrections above.

Revised all acceptance criteria to align with the docker/ directory convention required by architecture.md and ADR-004, incorporating all Architect and Tester feedback plus the human's explicit instruction. Changes: (1) AC #1 — corrected file path from repo root to docker/docker-compose.yml. (2) AC #3 — corrected working directory to docker/ (or -f flag from repo root); also updated AC #13 and AC #14 consistently. (3) AC #4 — added DATABASE_URL to the required .env.example keys per Architect note. (4) AC #6 — replaced vague 'reaches the database' with observable binary outcome: backend process does not exit with a connection error AND container logs confirm a successful database connection (or a health/readiness endpoint responds HTTP 200). (5) AC #7 — replaced vague 'can reach backend' with observable binary outcome: curl/wget from inside the frontend container to http://backend:<BACKEND_PORT> returns a non-connection-error response. (6) AC #8 — corrected Dockerfile paths from apps/frontend/Dockerfile and apps/backend/Dockerfile to docker/Dockerfile.frontend and docker/Dockerfile.backend. (7) AC #9 — corrected attribution from 'Dockerfile mounts' to 'compose file mounts' (bind-mounts are declared in compose, not Dockerfile). (8) AC #10 — same correction as AC #9. Updated Files Likely to Change section to reflect docker/ paths throughout. Previous Architect and Tester approvals are invalidated; re-approval required.

Revised acceptance criteria now correctly align with architecture.md (Structure section) and ADR-004 (Rule #4). All previous file-path and observability concerns have been addressed. One minor naming adjustment required: AC #2 and AC #6 reference the database service as 'db', but architecture.md > Structure > Directory Responsibilities specifies the compose service name as 'postgres'. The service name must be changed from 'db' to 'postgres' in AC #2 and AC #6 (and any other references) for consistency with the canonical architecture. This is a naming-only change; all other revised criteria are architecturally sound.

3 acceptance criteria remain untestable due to the Architect's outstanding naming correction (db -> postgres) not yet being applied. (1) AC #2: still names the database service 'db' — must be changed to 'postgres' per architecture.md and Architect's required correction. (2) AC #5: still refers to 'the db service' — must be changed to 'the postgres service'. (3) AC #6: still specifies hostname 'db' for Docker internal DNS — must be changed to 'postgres' since Docker Compose DNS resolves to the service name. All previously-flagged criteria (AC #1, #3, #7, #8, #9, #10) have been successfully resolved. Remaining criteria (AC #4, #11, #12, #13, #14, #15) are testable as written. Only the three db->postgres naming corrections are required before full approval.

Applied the remaining db->postgres service naming corrections required by the Architect's second 'Approved with notes' and confirmed by the Tester's second rejection. Changes: (1) AC #2 — changed service name from 'db' to 'postgres': 'The compose file defines exactly three services: frontend, backend, and postgres (PostgreSQL).' (2) AC #5 — changed 'The db service' to 'The postgres service': 'The postgres service uses the official postgres Docker image and is configured via the environment variables defined in .env.' (3) AC #6 — changed hostname reference from 'db' to 'postgres' and updated service reference accordingly: 'The backend service can successfully connect to the postgres service using the hostname postgres (Docker internal DNS)…'. All other criteria remain unchanged from the previous approved revision. Previous sign-offs are invalidated; re-approval from Architect and Tester required.

Final unconditional approval. All 15 revised acceptance criteria are fully aligned with the canonical architecture. Verified: AC #1 (docker/docker-compose.yml — Structure + ADR-004 Rule #4), AC #2 (services: frontend, backend, postgres — Directory Responsibilities), AC #3 (run from docker/ — Key Pattern #7), AC #4 (.env.example at repo root with DATABASE_URL — Key Pattern #6 + External Integrations), AC #5 (postgres service with official image — Directory Responsibilities + External Integrations), AC #6 (backend connects via hostname 'postgres' — consistent with service name), AC #7 (frontend reaches backend, observable — no conflict), AC #8 (docker/Dockerfile.frontend and docker/Dockerfile.backend — Structure + ADR-004), AC #9/#10 (bind-mount volumes in compose file — Key Pattern #7), AC #11 (named volume), AC #12 (user-defined network), AC #13 (docker compose down from docker/), AC #14 (README Local Development section), AC #15 (no hardcoded secrets — Key Pattern #6). Implementation constraints noted: postgres image version 16+, DATABASE_URL uses 'postgres' hostname, all Docker files exclusively in docker/.

Implemented Docker Compose local dev orchestration. Corrected service name db → postgres in docker/docker-compose.yml (service definition and depends_on), .env.example (DATABASE_URL hostname), and README.md (services table). Both Dockerfiles were already compliant. PR #20 opened.

decisions.approach: Existing docker/ skeleton files required only naming corrections (db → postgres) to reach full AC compliance. No structural changes to Dockerfiles were needed.
risks: Bind-mount volumes (../apps/frontend, ../apps/backend) will cause docker compose up to fail until the apps/ directory structure from Task #1 (monorepo setup) is merged.

Anonymous node_modules volumes prevent host bind-mount from shadowing container-installed packages — this is intentional but developers must be aware that npm install inside the container is the source of truth for dependencies.
